732	\begin{lemma}[Triple-overlap multiplicativity of the bundle transition (C2)]
733	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_mul}
734	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle}
735	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); this is the hard step of the bundle cocycle.
736	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_cocycle,
737	    lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
738	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:modules_pullbackComp}
739	  Over each triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\) the base-change transports
740	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three bundle transitions satisfy
741	  \[
742	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
743	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J},
744	  \]
745	  the module-level form of the descent condition (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for
746	  the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\). This is the \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued multiplicative cocycle
747	  of \(\mathcal{U}\).
748	\end{lemma}
749	\begin{proof}
750	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_cocycle,
751	    lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq, lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport,
752	    lem:gr_glueData_bridges, def:modules_pullbackComp}
753	  At the matrix level the assertion is the cocycle identity for the Cramer inverses,
754	  \[
755	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}
756	    \qquad \text{on } V_{IJK},
757	  \]
758	  which follows from the minor multiplicativity underlying the scheme-level cocycle
759	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} -- in the explicit matrix form
760	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq} -- together with the inverse identities
761	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}. To upgrade this matrix equation to the stated
762	  equality of sheaf-of-module isomorphisms, the three transitions
763	  \(g_{IJ}, g_{JK}, g_{IK}\), which live a priori on three different pairwise overlaps, are
764	  transported to the common triple overlap \(V_{IJK}\) by
765	  \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}; their source, middle, and target sheaves
766	  are matched up by the endpoint bridges \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}, and the iterated
767	  pullbacks are reassociated by the pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}.
768	  Because the biproduct assembly of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} turns matrix multiplication
769	  into composition of scalar-endomorphism components, the transported composite
770	  \(\widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}\) realises the left-hand matrix
771	  product and \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\) realises the right-hand side, so the matrix identity
772	  yields the module equality. The substantive difficulty is the endpoint bookkeeping: aligning
773	  the three transports' domains and codomains through the base-change transport and the bridges
774	  so that the composite typechecks against \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\).
775	\end{proof}

838	  \[
839	    g_{I,J}\, X^I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I \;=\; X^J \qquad \text{on } U^I_J,

841	  which is precisely the defining formula of the chart transition
842	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}).
843	  Hence the local maps \(u^I\), transported through the trivialisations of
844	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective
845	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-linear homomorphism
846	  \[
847	    u \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
848	      \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \mathcal{U},
849	  \]
850	  the \emph{tautological} (or \emph{universal}) rank-\(d\) quotient of the trivial
851	  rank-\(r\) sheaf. It is surjective because each \(u^I\) is (split) surjective and

853	  free of rank \(d\) by \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}. The pair
854	  \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is the family of rank-\(d\) quotients carried
855	  by \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself.

858	\section{The functor of points}
859	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
860	
861	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
862	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
863	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
864	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
865	identities that make that action functorial.
866	
867	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
868	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
869	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
870	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
871	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
872	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
873	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
874	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
875	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
876	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
877	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
878	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
879	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
880	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
881	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
882	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
883	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
884	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
885	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
886	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
887	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
888	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
889	  functor's value at \(T\).
890	
891	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
892	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
893	  \[
894	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
895	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
896	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
897	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
898	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
899	  \]
900	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
901	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
902	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
903	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
904	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
905	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
906	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
907	  map of equivalence classes.
908	\end{definition}

1085	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
1086	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
1087	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
1088	
1089	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
1090	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
1091	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
1092	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
1093	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
1094	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
1095	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
1096	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
1097	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
1098	  map_id X := by
1099	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
1100	    ext z
1101	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1102	    | _ x =>
1103	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
1104	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
1105	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
1106	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
1107	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
1108	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
1109	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
1110	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
1111	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1112	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
1113	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
1114	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
1115	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
1116	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
1117	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
1118	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
1119	    ext z
1120	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1121	    | _ x =>
1122	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
1123	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
1124	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
1125	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
1126	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
1127	      -- through `pullbackComp` — the composite analogue of the `map_id` obstacle.
1128	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
1129	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (g∘f) x).q` and `(pullbackComp.app x.F).symm.hom` (defeq), writing
1130	      -- the composite as `g.unop ≫ f.unop` so the `pullbackComp` naturality matches syntactically
1131	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1132	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (g.unop ≫ f.unop)).map x.q) ≫
1133	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app x.F
1134	        = (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x)).q
1135	      -- expose the `pullbackComp.inv` naturality square (mirrors the `map_id` reduction)
1136	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.naturality x.q]
1137	      -- the composite free coherence (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`) in inverse form: invert both
1138	      -- sides of the iso equation `pullbackComp.hom.app free ≫ pfba.hom = (pullback g).map pfa.hom
1139	      -- ≫ pfb.hom`.
1140	      have hstar : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫

1144	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv := by

1157	              Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map x.q) hstar).trans

1029	
1030	\leanok
1031	[The Grassmannian / Quot-of-trivial-sheaf functor \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\)]
1032	  \label{def:grassmannian_functor}
1033	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor}
1034	  \uses{def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:quot_functor, def:gr_rankQuotient,
1035	    lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1036	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1037	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
1038	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L557-L564).
1039	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1040	  % "represents the
1041	  %  contravariant functor
1042	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
1043	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
1044	  %  set of all equivalence classes
1045	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
1046	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$."
1047	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
1048	  The \emph{Grassmannian functor}
1049	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d) = \Quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}
1050	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/\mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) is the contravariant functor
1051	  from schemes to sets defined as follows. For a scheme \(T\), a \emph{family of
1052	  rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} is a pair
1053	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a locally free
1054	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\) of rank \(d\) together with a surjective
1055	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-linear homomorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1056	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\). Two such families
1057	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
1058	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
1059	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
1060	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
1061	  \[
1062	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
1063	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
1064	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
1065	  \]
1066	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
1067	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
1068	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
1069	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
1070	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
1071	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
1072	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
1073	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
1074	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
1075	\end{definition}
